parameterGroups.label: Playback Control
parameterGroups.rationale: Toggle parameters that control voice allocation and retriggering behavior, independent of envelope shape

parameters.name: Monophonic
parameters.description: Enables monophonic mode where only one voice is rendered

parameters.name: Retrigger
parameters.description: Restarts the envelope when a new note is triggered in monophonic mode

parameterGroups.label: Envelope Stages
parameterGroups.rationale: Time and level parameters defining the five AHDSR stages, each potentially modulated per-voice

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.description: Controls how long the envelope takes to reach full level after a note-on

parameters.name: AttackLevel
parameters.description: The peak level reached at the end of the attack phase

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.description: The time the envelope stays at the attack level before decaying

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: The time the envelope takes to fall from the attack level to the sustain level

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: The level maintained while the note is held

parameters.name: Release
parameters.description: The time the envelope takes to fall from the sustain level to zero after note-off

parameterGroups.label: Curve Shapes
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters controlling the curvature of attack and decay/release transitions

parameters.name: AttackCurve
parameters.description: Controls the curvature of the attack phase (0.0 = concave, 1.0 = convex)

parameters.name: DecayCurve
parameters.description: Controls the curvature of the decay and release phases

parameterGroups.label: Performance
parameterGroups.rationale: CPU optimization toggle that trades accuracy for performance

parameters.name: EcoMode
parameters.description: Enables 16x downsampling for reduced CPU usage

gaps.question: What is the exact stage calculation order in renderNextBlock/calculateBlock? How does the envelope transition between A->H->D->S->R states, and what is the per-sample calculation formula for each stage?
gaps.context: The reference page pseudo-code needs to show the correct state machine and per-sample computation for each stage

gaps.question: What do AttackCurve and DecayCurve values actually produce? Is this an exponential coefficient, a power curve, or some other interpolation? What curve does 0.0 vs 0.5 vs 1.0 produce?
gaps.context: Users need to understand how the curve knobs affect the envelope shape to design sounds accurately

gaps.question: AttackLevel and Sustain are in Decibel mode (-100 to 0 dB). How is the dB-to-linear conversion applied? Is -100 dB treated as true silence (0.0) or a very small value?
gaps.context: Determines whether 0 dB sustain means unity gain and whether -100 dB is effectively zero

gaps.question: How do Monophonic and Retrigger interact? When Monophonic=On and Retrigger=On, does the envelope restart from zero or from its current value? What happens when Retrigger=Off - does the envelope continue from its current position?
gaps.context: Critical for legato playing behavior documentation

gaps.question: The DecayCurve description says it controls 'the curvature of the decay and release phases'. Does a single DecayCurve value truly apply to both decay and release identically, or are there differences?
gaps.context: Users need to know if they can independently shape decay vs release curves (they cannot in AHDSR, unlike FlexAHDSR)

gaps.question: How does EcoMode's 16x downsampling work? Does it calculate one sample and hold for 16, or does it interpolate? What artifacts can this introduce (e.g., zipper noise on fast attacks)?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the CPU/quality tradeoff to decide when EcoMode is appropriate

gaps.question: Hold has no modulation chain unlike the other time parameters. Is this intentional? Is there a technical reason Hold cannot be modulated per-voice?
gaps.context: Worth noting in the reference page if Hold is deliberately unmodulatable

gaps.question: All modulation chains use gain mode. For time parameters (Attack, Decay, Release), does gain mode mean the modulator value multiplies the time directly (e.g., mod=0.5 halves the time)? For level parameters (AttackLevel, Sustain in dB), how does gain-mode multiplication interact with the decibel scale?
gaps.context: Users adding velocity modulators to these chains need to know the scaling behavior

notes: AHDSR is the most commonly used envelope in HISE. Key observations: (1) Sustain and AttackLevel use Decibel mode (-100 to 0 dB) while time parameters use milliseconds. (2) DecayCurve is shared between decay and release phases - FlexAHDSR separates these. (3) Hold has no modulation chain, unlike Attack/Decay/Sustain/Release. (4) EcoMode defaults to On (1.0), suggesting downsampling is the recommended default. (5) Monophonic default is 'dynamic' (likely determined by parent context). (6) All five mod chains are VoiceStartModulator-constrained with gain mode, meaning they scale parameter values at note-on time. Cross-reference: FlexAHDSR is the enhanced version with separate ReleaseCurve, NormalizedPercentage sustain, and DisplayBufferSource interface.
